  "findings": [
    "Sheave misalignment detected using straightedge check",
    "Excessive axial or angular offset exceeding manufacturer limits"
  ],
  "actions": [
    "Loosen sheave mounting bolts and adjust axial position along the shaft",
    "Re-align sheaves using a straightedge or laser alignment tool until four-point contact is achieved",
    "Torque bushings/bolts to manufacturer specifications and re-verify alignment"
  ],
  "rationale": "The straightedge check reveals sheave misalignment that exceeds standard manufacturer tolerances (typically less than 0.5 degrees or 1/10 inch per foot of center distance). This will cause premature belt wear, vibration, and potential bearing failure if not corrected before commissioning.",
  "references": [
    "Manufacturer Belt Drive Installation and Maintenance Manual",
    "MPTTA (Mechanical Power Transmission Association) Belt Drive Guidelines"
  ]
}
